agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the technical architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to turn these specs into production-ready code with tests.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"Calling transitionState(order, PENDING) on an order with status SHIPPED must throw/return an ORDER_STATE_INVALID error and leave the order status unchanged"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"Calling issueRefund(orderId) when the order status is anything other than DELIVERED must be rejected; only orders with status DELIVERED may have refunds issued against them"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose a PATCH /orders/{id}/state endpoint for state transitions","implementation_note":"The endpoint receives the target state in the request body. It delegates to OrderStateMachine for validation before persisting. Return 409 Conflict with error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID when a transition is rejected."},{"decision":"State transitions validated in an OrderStateMachine class","implementation_note":"OrderStateMachine owns the full map of allowed transitions. It is a pure domain logic class with no I/O dependencies, making it straightforward to unit test. Every transition not explicitly allowed is denied by default (allowlist, not denylist)."},{"decision":"Refund logic lives in a separate RefundService","implementation_note":"RefundService calls the order service (or queries order state directly) to verify the order is in DELIVERED state before issuing a refund. This keeps refund orchestration decoupled from state machine logic. If the order is not DELIVERED, RefundService must reject the request before any payment-side effects occur."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Reject SHIPPED to PENDING transition","given":"An order exists with status SHIPPED","when":"A PATCH /orders/{id}/state request is sent with target state PENDING","then":"The API responds with HTTP 409 and error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID; the order remains in SHIPPED state"},{"criterion":"Refund only on DELIVERED order","given":"An order exists with status SHIPPED (or any non-DELIVERED status)","when":"A refund is requested for that order via RefundService","then":"The refund is rejected and no payment-side effects occur"},{"criterion":"Refund succeeds on DELIVERED order","given":"An order exists with status DELIVERED","when":"A refund is requested for that order via RefundService","then":"The refund is issued successfully"},{"criterion":"Disallowed transitions are denied by default","given":"An OrderStateMachine with an explicit allowlist of valid transitions","when":"Any transition not in the allowlist is attempted","then":"The transition is rejected with ORDER_STATE_INVALID"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Order state invariant","technical_term":"Transition guard in OrderStateMachine","definition":"A business rule that prohibits certain state transitions. Enforced in code as a guard check inside OrderStateMachine before any state mutation is persisted."},{"domain_term":"Revert-to-pending","technical_term":"transitionState(order, PENDING)","definition":"An attempt to move an order back to PENDING status. Bird's domain rules prohibit this from SHIPPED state; the state machine must enforce this."},{"domain_term":"ORDER_STATE_INVALID","technical_term":"Error code returned in 409 response","definition":"The canonical error code used when a disallowed state transition is attempted. Used consistently across the API response and any domain exceptions."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are unambiguous and directly testable","Architecture decisions from MJ align cleanly with Bird's invariants","Acceptance criteria map 1:1 to stated domain rules"],"low_confidence_areas":["Full set of valid state transitions not specified — only two invariants given; Shaq may need the complete transition graph to implement OrderStateMachine's allowlist","Refund failure behavior (error code, HTTP status) not explicitly specified by Bird or MJ for the refund-rejected case"],"assumptions":["HTTP 409 Conflict is the appropriate status for rejected state transitions (not specified explicitly by MJ but implied by the conflict semantics)","OrderStateMachine uses an allowlist pattern — transitions not explicitly permitted are denied","RefundService rejects refunds synchronously before any payment side-effects, not via eventual consistency"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the technical architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to turn these specs into production-ready code with tests.","domain_rules"

Bird's domain rules prohibit this from SHIPPED state; the state machine must enforce this."},{"domain_term":"ORDER_STATE_INVALID","technical_term":"Error code returned in 409 response","definition":"The canonical error code used when a disallowed state transition is attempted.
